pattern: Finding the tightest container that still fits an item is a single linear scan tracking the best-so-far candidate, breaking ties by preferring the earliest index found rather than re-scanning or sorting.
hints: You don't need to sort anything -- walk the array once and keep the index of the smallest capacity seen so far that is still >= itemSize.
When a new candidate ties the current best capacity, keep the earlier index (only replace on a strictly smaller capacity).
If no box was ever large enough after the full scan, the answer is -1.

edge_inputs.description: several boxes qualify, pick the smallest capacity among them
duplicate minimal qualifying capacity, earliest index wins
exact matches present alongside larger ones -- must not pick a larger box over an exact-fit earlier one
capacities strictly decreasing, the qualifying box is last
itemSize larger than every capacity except the very last, smallest index rule still applies among equal minima

stress.description: random capacities and item sizes across the full constraint range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    capacity = [rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    itemSize = rng.randint(1, 100)
    return [capacity, itemSize]
